paper_title: Experimental Design and Trackmaking in Sediment Substrates

methods.description: A virtual three-dimensional mesh was constructed using a series of pictures taken from different angles of the silicone cast and processed using the software 123D Catch. The generated files were analyzed with the 3D design software Rhinoceros to study the sediment layers and footprint formations.

steps.description: A virtual three-dimensional mesh was constructed using a series of pictures taken from different angles of the silicone cast and processed using the software 123D Catch.
steps.searchable_summary: construct 3D mesh from images using 123D Catch
inputs: series of pictures of silicone cast

steps.description: Generated files were coded with 'obj' formats and analyzed with the 3D design software Rhinoceros.

methods.description: A set of nine experiments was performed to test different scenarios regarding footprint formation using varying water contents in sediments and different human trackmakers.

steps.description: A 230 cm long, 80 cm wide and 10 cm deep box was constructed and placed on a flat surface under shadow to avoid desiccation. It was filled up with ~0.18 m3 of sediments extracted directly from the layer near the excavated ichnite on grid 14AD.
steps.searchable_summary: construct and fill experimental trackway box with sediment

steps.description: Trackmakers walked on the sediment immediately after setting up the box, with a maximum of a 15-minutes pause in between for: 1) taking pictures with scale, 2) measuring the hallux, heel and medial longitudinal arch depths of each footprint with a measuring tape and a ruler serving as a surface level, and 3) flattening of the trackbed surface.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct trackmaking and measure footprint characteristics

steps.description: Subsequent tests were made after mixing 12 and 38 l of additional water, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: adjust water content in sediment and repeat tests

methods.description: Before each experimental trackway, sediment samples were analyzed for their water content to categorize them as 'dry', 'wet', or 'saturated'.

steps.description: Data on sample weight before and after total desiccation after 48 hrs. at 40˚C, indicated 36%, 44% and 68% water content on each sediment trial.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze sediment water content by weight difference

methods.description: Walking speed was estimated using Alexander's formula to compare the walking effort across different substrate conditions.

steps.description: Walking speed was estimated using Alexander's formula noted as: Speed = 0.25 g^0.5 x SL^1.67 x H^-1.17, where g is the gravitational acceleration (9.81 m/s^2), SL is the stride length, and H the hip height.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate walking speed using Alexander's formula
